title: Pin-operator diagnosis: compile-time constant de-pinning for performance
prompt: Given this query: `@active_statuses ["approved", "published"]; from p in Post, where: p.status in ^@active_statuses`. Explain in a code comment what tradeoff pinning the module attribute creates for Postgres query planning (parameterized value prevents index selectivity optimization), and rewrite it to NOT pin the compile-time list so the query is statically `WHERE status IN ('approved', 'published')`. Write the rewritten function to `lib/my_app/blog.ex` with a comment that documents the reasoning. Use `Enum.map` + unquote or the `^` -> bare list approach via `where: fragment(?, ...)` alternative, or just inline the list literal in the query — whatever produces a static WHERE clause.
tier_rationale: Requires understanding WHEN to AVOID the pin, which is the inverse of the usual rule. Only applies to compile-time constants; tricky nuance.
research_citation: hubertlepicki Elixir Forum: 'Without the pin the query should not use a parameter for the @active_statuses, but include the value in the query itself. The change to not using the pin operator has a profound overall impact on the performance.'
